subject: aefe2954995877480
findings: All three Qodo round-1 findings fixed red-first; final counts Pester 90/90, probes 12/12, Verify-Verdict.ps1 exit 0 (25/25) at commit b10c1520844fffe29161d0ddbb77be1628a3db33; one collateral test-harness bug found and fixed (disclosed) to make Q3's nonzero-exit criterion observable, proven harness-only by comparing against a genuinely separate pwsh -File process.
abstract:
|
  Executed the three owner-ruled Qodo findings in the same worktree/branch as the prior Copilot cycle, base-verified clean at 0478afa62efd0cce1beae77e819906dc96a208ca. Q3 (path traversal) followed full red-first: two new Producer.Tests.ps1 cases (agent_id '../evil' and 'a/b') failed against the unfixed script with the stated reason (exit 0, i.e. it wrote), with a manual reproduction pinpointing the actual escaped write location outside the store; sanitized $subject against an allowlist regex plus an explicit '..' substring check before the only Join-Path use, routed through the existing Law-4 Add-Fault path. Fixing it surfaced a genuine, disclosed, pre-existing test-harness defect (Invoke-Producer's in-process invocation let an uncaught terminating Write-Error crash the Pester block instead of reporting an exit code) which I proved was harness-only (a real separate pwsh -File process already exited 1 correctly) and fixed with a minimal try/catch, without altering the other 8 pre-existing tests' behavior. Q4 removed the tracked .pyc and gitignored __pycache__/. Q5 guarded both probe hooks with a command -v python check mirroring the producer hooks' command -v pwsh shape, with a LOUD note (not silent, unlike the producer hooks) since a probe log gap is an invisible instrumentation loss rather than a graceful degrade; fault-injection (PATH stripped of python, sanity-checked to genuinely fail command -v python) and normal-path runs were both observed directly. One commit, b10c1520844fffe29161d0ddbb77be1628a3db33, 6 files (5 modified, 1 deleted), never pushed.
